reasoning: ChargePoint’s financial position as of May 5, 2025, makes a bankruptcy filing by July 1, 2025, highly unlikely. According to its Q4 FY2025 results (ending Jan 31, 2025) reported on March 4, 2025, the company held $225 million in cash and has an undrawn $150 million revolving credit facility. Its annual cash burn has significantly decreased to approximately $147 million, suggesting a runway of over 1.5 years. Furthermore, the company successfully extended the maturity of its $300 million convertible notes to April 2028, removing any immediate debt wall. While the stock hit a 52-week low in April 2025 and some analysts expressed concerns about market conditions, the lack of immediate liquidity pressure or near-term debt maturities makes a Chapter 11 filing within the next eight weeks improbable. No reports of a bankruptcy petition or imminent filing appeared in news through early May 2025.
